prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `e39e9282`.
codex_response:
Implemented `e39e9282` end-to-end. The rule is: each `9` sits one cell outside a neighboring `3x3` block; for `6` blocks it gets projected two cells inward and replaces part of the block, while for `5` blocks the whole block is erased and each `9` is duplicated one cell inward. I discarded the summary hints and documented the mismatch in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e39e9282/notes.md); no new primitive was needed.

The generator stays close to the official family by using only background `8`, solid `3x3` blocks of `5`/`6`, and side-adjacent `9` markers, with varied grid sizes and 1-6 well-separated blocks. Official verification passed `4/4`, the build produced 1000 examples, generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`, and the rendered samples looked on-distribution in a spot check.
